text: Your ideal workweek rhythm is…

options.text: Long quiet blocks; meetings are exceptions.
Lots of co-working time and live discussions.
Predictable rituals: standups, docs, handoffs.
Plans change often; I adapt day by day.

text: When a project starts, you first want…

options.text: A written brief and owners for each piece.
A kickoff that gets everyone aligned emotionally.
Freedom to explore before commitments.
A whiteboard session to shape the idea together.

text: Feedback that helps you most sounds like…

options.text: “Here’s exactly what to change next time.”
“Here’s how this landed for the team.”
“Take another pass when you’re in flow.”
“Let’s try a quick experiment and see.”

text: Your nightmare workday includes…

options.text: Constant pings with no time to think.
Silent solo work all day with no collaboration.
Chaos: no roles, no written decisions.
Rigid process when the situation already changed.

text: In meetings, you naturally…

options.text: Take notes and turn chaos into next steps.
Make sure quieter voices are heard.
Prefer async updates instead of another call.
Push for a decision and move on.

text: You feel most trusted when a manager…

options.text: Sets clear outcomes and gets out of the way.
Checks in on how you’re doing, not just tasks.
Protects your calendar for deep work.
Creates space for the team to ideate together.

text: Under a tight deadline, you…

options.text: Cut scope and ship something real.
Lock in a plan and execute calmly.
Go heads-down and minimize noise.
Pull the right people in to unblock fast.

text: Your favorite collaboration setup is…

options.text: Shared docs with clear owners.
Live calls and real-time brainstorming.
Async threads; reply on my own time.
Minimal tools; I own the outcome end-to-end.

text: Conflict on the team makes you…

options.text: Step in to clarify facts and next steps.
Talk privately to rebuild trust first.
Prefer to step away until it’s calmer.
Address it quickly so we don’t stall.

text: You’d rather be evaluated on…

options.text: Reliable delivery against commitments.
The quality of relationships you build.
Impact you drove with autonomy.
Creativity and ideas brought to the group.

text: “Remote vs office” for you is really about…

options.text: Controlling interruptions and deep work.
Energy from being around people.
Freedom to structure my own day.
Whatever helps the team move fastest today.

text: Your career happiness spikes when…

options.text: You can own a problem end-to-end.
The team feels safe and aligned.
The system runs smoothly without heroics.
You’re learning fast in a changing environment.

text: When you learn something new for work, you prefer to…

options.text: Read quietly and practice alone first.
Pair with someone and learn by doing together.
Follow a structured course or checklist.
Jump into a real task and fix gaps as you go.

text: A vague goal with no metrics feels…

options.text: Fine if I can carve focus time to figure it out.
Exciting if we can workshop it as a group.
Uncomfortable until we define owners and milestones.
Normal; clarity can emerge while we move.

text: Your default reaction to a new company process is…

options.text: “Will this protect my focus time?”
“How will this affect team vibes?”
“Show me the RACI and the template.”
“We’ll probably rewrite it in two weeks anyway.”

text: The best praise you can get is…

options.text: “You delivered—no drama.”
“You made the team feel unstuck.”
“Your doc made everything obvious.”
“Your idea changed the room.”

text: After a stressful week, you recharge by…

options.text: Long offline time with zero work chat.
Talking it out with a trusted colleague.
Tidying your tasks and inbox so Monday is clean.
Starting something new to regain momentum.

text: Your ideal team size for daily work is…

options.text: Small enough that I can own a slice solo.
Big enough for energy, small enough to know everyone.
Large if roles and interfaces are clear.
Whatever ships fastest for this phase.

text: You get skeptical when leadership says…

options.text: “We’ll figure out the details later.” (with no owner)
“Just be available all day on Slack.”
“Process for process’s sake.”
“Don’t worry about how people feel.”

text: In a brainstorm, you’re most likely to…

options.text: Build on others’ ideas in real time.
Ask the question that names the tradeoff.
Stay quiet until you have one sharp idea.
Suggest a cheap test before a big debate.

text: You respect a coworker who…

options.text: Doesn’t need hand-holding and still communicates clearly.
Makes hard conversations feel safer.
Writes decisions down so nobody is guessing.
Turns ambiguity into a prototype.

text: Your “productive morning” usually means…

options.text: Two hours of uninterrupted work before chat.
A quick sync to align the team’s energy.
Checking the plan and updating the board.
Picking the highest-leverage move and switching fast.

text: If you disagree with a popular plan, you…

options.text: Write a clear alternative with tradeoffs.
Talk to key people 1:1 before the room decides.
Opt out of the debate and prove it with work.
Challenge it live so the group can react.

text: “Scope creep” to you sounds like…

options.text: A focus problem unless someone says no.
A people problem if expectations weren’t shared.
A planning problem: change control needed.
Sometimes the signal that we should pivot.

text: You feel most productive in a workplace that…

options.text: Lets me block deep work without guilt.
Has lively collaboration spaces.
Has clear norms and written agreements.
Rewards speed of learning over perfection.

text: Your 1:1 with a manager should mostly be about…

options.text: Removing blockers and clarifying ownership.
How you’re doing as a human, not only output.
Priorities, timelines, and expectations.
What experiments to run next week.

text: When documentation is missing, you…

options.text: Create the minimum doc so future-you survives.
Pull people together to align verbally first.
Prefer to stay in flow and document later.
Ship something small and document what broke.

text: You’re happiest when your role is…

options.text: A clear lane you can run end-to-end.
A glue role that helps others succeed.
A stabilizer who makes the machine work.
A scout in changing terrain.

text: “Good collaboration” to you means…

options.text: Fewer meetings, clearer handoffs.
Psychological safety and honest dialogue.
Shared definitions of done and who decides what.
Rapid feedback loops and quick iterations.

text: If you had to pick your strongest professional edge, it would be…

options.text: Sustained focus on hard problems
Building trust and helping people feel heard
Owning outcomes without needing oversight
Staying adaptable when everything shifts
